notes: Core direct contribution: the source adapts and uses a random track generator to create four tracks. This is not a supporting fixed-course transfer; no fixed-route-only claim is used for retention.
deciding_source_fact: At p. 12, Appendix B, "Experiment design," the paper states that it adapts a random track generator for the F1TENTH simulator and generates four new tracks, one for training and three for validation.
protocol_reasoning: This is source-native, directly inspected evidence that the report implements and uses a generator emitting course realizations, satisfying the v8 core condition for a direct generated-course method. The track-boundary and racing-line control discussion elsewhere is fixed-course control context, not the basis for retention; it is not characterized as a generator.
